title: Devteam implementation journey — ADR-0128 §12 (WF-0064)
note: The 8-step journey every governed code change follows: Classify -> Model -> Decide -> Compile -> Implement -> Verify -> Review -> Receipt. Model runs only when the resolved profile requires it; Decide declares that implementation must not proceed while a material Decision is missing (declared here, enforced by WF-0067); Receipt compares planned vs executed. Shadow-only at WF-0064.

steps.title: Classify
steps.summary: Resolve CMIS/DAS/profile from the Request Intent Envelope (WF-0063). Business, Work Nature and Ceremony are inherited, never recalculated.

steps.title: Model
steps.summary: Make the domain model explicit (bounded contexts, invariants, state authority). Never runs for simple/modular work (proportionality).

steps.title: Decide
steps.summary: A material change (contract, state authority, dependency adoption) needs a governing accepted Decision before implementation. Declared gate; blocking arrives with WF-0067.

steps.title: Compile
steps.summary: Compile the bounded implementation packet (scope, contracts, files, checks) the implementer must respect.

steps.title: Implement
steps.summary: Smallest safe diff for the packet; tests written with the code; deviations recorded.

steps.title: Verify
steps.summary: Run the declared checks; behavior-level evidence, receipts not assertions.

steps.title: Review
steps.summary: Packet-vs-diff review when the implementation-review skill fired (non-trivial, high-risk, public contract or domain-driven+).

steps.title: Receipt
steps.summary: Record planned-vs-executed: agents/skills selected vs actually applied (§18), deviations, evidence tier.
